inline_review:
commit 6c4733df128cee4562919555daa45e7b7d9e8086
Author: Byungchul Park <byungchul@sk.com>
Date:   Mon Mar 16 22:31:13 2026 +0000

    mm: introduce a new page type for page pool in page type

    This commit changes the page pool condition to use a new page_type API
    (PGTY_netpp) instead of checking page->pp_magic. It also adds the
    page_type field to struct net_iov at the same offset as in struct page
    so the page_type APIs can be shared.

    Link: https://lore.kernel.org/all/582f41c0-2742-4400-9c81-0d46bf4e8314@gmail.com/

> diff --git a/net/core/page_pool.c b/net/core/page_pool.c
> index 265a729431bb..877bbf7a1938 100644
> --- a/net/core/page_pool.c
> +++ b/net/core/page_pool.c
[ ... ]
> @@ -702,8 +702,18 @@ s32 page_pool_inflight(const struct page_pool *pool, bool strict)
>  
>  void page_pool_set_pp_info(struct page_pool *pool, netmem_ref netmem)
>  {
> +	struct page *page;
> +
>  	netmem_set_pp(netmem, pool);
> -	netmem_or_pp_magic(netmem, PP_SIGNATURE);
> +
> +	/* XXX: Now that the offset of page_type is shared between
> +	 * struct page and net_iov, just cast the netmem to struct page
> +	 * unconditionally by clearing NET_IOV if any, no matter whether
> +	 * it comes from struct net_iov or struct page.  This should be
> +	 * adjusted once the offset is no longer shared.
> +	 */
> +	page = (struct page *)((__force unsigned long)netmem & ~NET_IOV);
> +	__SetPageNetpp(page);

Will this trigger a kernel panic on systems with CONFIG_DEBUG_VM enabled
when called on a freshly allocated net_iov?

The __SetPageNetpp() macro strictly asserts that the memory is currently
untyped: VM_BUG_ON_PAGE(data_race(page->page_type) != UINT_MAX, page).
For standard pages, the buddy allocator automatically initializes the
overlapped _mapcount to -1 (UINT_MAX).

However, struct net_iov arrays are allocated dynamically. For example,
in io_uring/zcrx.c they are allocated with __GFP_ZERO, meaning page_type
is initialized to 0. In net/core/devmem.c they are allocated without
__GFP_ZERO, leaving page_type uninitialized. Neither allocator initializes
page_type to UINT_MAX.

Should the net_iov->page_type field be explicitly initialized to UINT_MAX
upon allocation to satisfy the page type API assertions?
output:
{"findings":[{"problem":"Missing `UINT_MAX` initialization of the `page_type` field in dynamically allocated `struct net_iov` arrays causes a `VM_BUG_ON_PAGE` kernel panic during page pool operations.","severity":"High","severity_explanation":"The commit transitions the page pool signature checking from `pp_magic` to the MM page type subsystem by adding an `unsigned int page_type` field to `struct net_iov`. To use the shared page type APIs, `page_pool_set_pp_info()` calls `__SetPageNetpp(page)` where `page` is a casted `net_iov`.

The macro `__SetPageNetpp()` strictly asserts that the memory is currently untyped: `VM_BUG_ON_PAGE(data_race(page->page_type) != UINT_MAX, page)`. For standard pages, the buddy allocator automatically initializes `_mapcount` (which overlays `page_type`) to `-1` (`UINT_MAX`).

However, `struct net_iov` arrays are allocated dynamically via `kvmalloc_objs()`. In `io_uring/zcrx.c` (`io_zcrx_create_ifq`), they are allocated with `__GFP_ZERO`, meaning `page_type` is initialized to `0`. In `net/core/devmem.c` (`net_devmem_bind_dmabuf`), they are allocated without `__GFP_ZERO` (leaving `page_type` uninitialized). Neither allocator initializes `page_type` to `UINT_MAX`.

When `page_pool_set_pp_info()` is called on a freshly allocated `net_iov`, the uninitialized or zeroed `page_type` causes the `page_type != UINT_MAX` assertion to fail, triggering an immediate kernel panic on systems with `CONFIG_DEBUG_VM` enabled. Furthermore, without `CONFIG_DEBUG_VM`, reading uninitialized memory could randomly cause `PageNetpp()` to erroneously return true before assignment. The `niov->page_type` field must be explicitly initialized to `UINT_MAX` upon allocation."}],"fixes":"","review_inline":"commit 6c4733df128cee4562919555daa45e7b7d9e8086
Author: Byungchul Park <byungchul@sk.com>

    Link: https://lore.kernel.org/all/582f41c0-2742-4400-9c81-0d46bf4e8314@gmail.com/

[ ... ]
> @@ -702,8 +702,18 @@ s32 page_pool_inflight(const struct page_pool *pool, bool strict)
>

> +	 */
> +	page = (struct page *)((__force unsigned long)netmem & ~NET_IOV);
> +	__SetPageNetpp(page);

Will this trigger a kernel panic on systems with CONFIG_DEBUG_VM enabled
when called on a freshly allocated net_iov?

The __SetPageNetpp() macro strictly asserts that the memory is currently
untyped: VM_BUG_ON_PAGE(data_race(page->page_type) != UINT_MAX, page).
For standard pages, the buddy allocator automatically initializes the
overlapped _mapcount to -1 (UINT_MAX).

However, struct net_iov arrays are allocated dynamically. For example,
in io_uring/zcrx.c they are allocated with __GFP_ZERO, meaning page_type
is initialized to 0. In net/core/devmem.c they are allocated without

page_type to UINT_MAX.

Should the net_iov->page_type field be explicitly initialized to UINT_MAX
upon allocation to satisfy the page type API assertions?
